name: event-horizon-vscode
description: Multi-agent orchestration for AI coding agents. One agent creates the plan, spawns the team, and manages the project. Shared plans, file locking, inter-agent messaging, budget controls, and live visualization. Works with Claude Code, OpenCode, Copilot, and Cursor.

eventHorizon.port.description: Port for the local event server that receives agent hooks. Requires extension restart.

eventHorizon.fileLockingEnabled.description: Enable file locking to prevent multiple agents from writing to the same file simultaneously. When enabled, agents must acquire a lock before writing — conflicting writes are blocked. Requires reinstalling hooks.

eventHorizon.worktreeIsolation.description: Enable git worktree isolation for spawned agents. Each agent gets its own copy of the repository — eliminates file conflicts entirely. Works with any git host (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket). Disable if you don't want extra branches.

eventHorizon.fontSize.description: UI font size for the Event Horizon panel. Affects all text in the universe and operations views.

eventHorizon.defaultView.description: Which view to show when opening Event Horizon.

eventHorizon.planShowAllColumns.description: Show all Kanban columns in the Plan tab, including empty ones.

eventHorizon.achievementsEnabled.description: Enable achievement medals and toast notifications.

eventHorizon.agentColors.claudeCode.description: Planet color for Claude Code agents.

eventHorizon.agentColors.copilot.description: Planet color for GitHub Copilot agents.

eventHorizon.agentColors.unknown.description: Planet color for unrecognized agent types.

eventHorizon.agentSizes.claudeCode.description: Planet size multiplier for Claude Code agents.

eventHorizon.agentSizes.copilot.description: Planet size multiplier for GitHub Copilot agents.

eventHorizon.agentSizes.opencode.description: Planet size multiplier for OpenCode agents.

eventHorizon.agentSizes.cursor.description: Planet size multiplier for Cursor agents.

eventHorizon.agentSizes.unknown.description: Planet size multiplier for unrecognized agent types.

enumDescriptions: Keep terminal in background after spawning
Focus the terminal immediately after spawning
Focus the terminal when the agent needs interaction (waiting state)
eventHorizon.spawnTerminalFocus.description: When spawning an agent via the orchestrator, how to handle terminal focus.

skills.description: Skill names to recommend for this role

instructions.description: Markdown instructions sent to the agent when assigned this role

eventHorizon.roles.custom.markdownDescription: Define custom agent roles beyond the 6 built-in ones (researcher, planner, implementer, reviewer, tester, debugger). Each role maps to skills and instructions.

eventHorizon.roles.assignments.markdownDescription: Map role IDs to default agent types. Example: `{ "researcher": "claude-code", "reviewer": "copilot" }`

eventHorizon.contextOptimizer.threshold.description: Token threshold for context optimization suggestions. A notification appears when instruction files (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, etc.) exceed this estimated token count.

eventHorizon.knowledge.autoDiscover.description: Scan workspace instruction files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, copilot-instructions.md, .claude/rules/*.md) on activation and on file changes, then surface them in the Knowledge tab tagged as source=auto. Auto entries are assigned a tier (L1 for root instructions, L2 for rules) and never overwrite user- or agent-authored entries. Disable if you only want knowledge written explicitly by agents or via the UI.

eventHorizon.budgetWarningThreshold.description: Budget warning threshold (0.1 to 1.0). A warning is shown when spending reaches this percentage of the plan budget.

eventHorizon.persistence.enabled.description: Enable SQLite persistence for events and knowledge. Disable to run in ephemeral mode (events lost on reload).

eventHorizon.persistence.retentionDays.description: Number of days to retain persisted events before auto-pruning on startup.

eventHorizon.websocket.enabled.description: Enable WebSocket endpoint at /ws for bidirectional agent communication. External tools can connect to ws://127.0.0.1:<port>/ws and receive debounced event broadcasts.

eventHorizon.contextGauge.enabled.description: Show context usage gauge (270° arc) on planet visuals. Color shifts cyan → amber → red as agents approach their context window limit.

eventHorizon.contextGauge.windowSize.description: Estimated context window size in tokens (drives the planet fuel-gauge calculation). Default is 200000 for Claude Sonnet/Opus.

eventHorizon.autoDetect.enabled.description: On extension activation, scan PATH for installed AI agent CLIs (claude, opencode, cursor) and offer one-click hook setup if any are found without configured hooks.

eventHorizon.watchdog.timeoutMinutes.description: Auto-fail spawned workers that have not emitted any event for this many minutes (likely stuck on a permission prompt or hung). Orchestrators are notified via the message queue. Set to 0 to disable. Interactive-mode workers are always excluded.

eventHorizon.projectGraph.enabled.description: Enable workspace project graph (code structure + docs + agent activity). The graph is built only on user invocation; nothing runs in the background.

eventHorizon.projectGraph.maxFiles.description: Cap on file count for a workspace scan. Workspaces over this size require manual confirmation.

eventHorizon.projectGraph.allowAgentLLME.description: Allow agents to add INFERRED nodes via the eh_extract_concepts MCP tool. EH itself never makes outbound model calls.

eventHorizon.projectGraph.maxFileSizeKb.description: Skip files larger than this size (KB) during workspace scans. Defaults to 256 KB — comfortably fits hand-written source while excluding inline-bundled vendor scripts that explode the graph with useless nodes.

eventHorizon.projectGraph.canvasMaxNodes.description: Maximum number of nodes to render in the Project Graph canvas at once. The canvas uses Barnes-Hut layout with viewport culling, so even thousands of nodes stay responsive — but enormous monorepos (50k+ nodes) can still saturate the SVG renderer. Lower this if pan/zoom feels sluggish; raise it if you need to see the full graph.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Event Horizon
walkthroughs.description: Set up your AI coding agent universe in 5 steps.

steps.title: Open the Universe
steps.description:
Open the Event Horizon panel from the activity bar.

[Open Universe](command:eventHorizon.open)

steps.title: Connect an Agent
steps.description:
Install hooks for Claude Code, OpenCode, or GitHub Copilot.

[Connect Claude Code](command:eventHorizon.setupClaudeCode)

steps.title: Explore the Universe
steps.description: Planets, ships, lightning arcs, astronauts — discover the cosmic visualization.

steps.title: Use the Command Center
steps.description: Select planets, view metrics, control agents, and earn achievements.

steps.title: Manage Agent Skills
steps.description: Discover, create, organize, and browse skills from the marketplace.

scripts.build: node scripts/build-extension.mjs && node scripts/copy-sql-wasm.mjs && node scripts/copy-tree-sitter-wasm.mjs && pnpm run build:webview
scripts.build:ext: node scripts/build-extension.mjs && node scripts/copy-sql-wasm.mjs && node scripts/copy-tree-sitter-wasm.mjs
scripts.build:ext:prod: node scripts/build-extension.mjs --prod && node scripts/copy-sql-wasm.mjs && node scripts/copy-tree-sitter-wasm.mjs
scripts.package:vsix: pnpm run build:ext:prod && pnpm run build:webview:prod && vsce package --no-dependencies
